states:
Environment: Google Slides presentation editor, with a secondary overlay panel on the right that appears to be from a separate application or prototype interface.

The slide being composed is a title slide for a project called "AudienceSymbiosis," styled in a bold handwritten/marker typeface underlined for emphasis, with a cream background. Below the title sits a subtitle describing it as "A sociocultural affordance, human-centered, adapter system to enhance user creativity and prototyp[ing]" — the text is cut off by the overlay. Two supporting visuals anchor the lower half: a photograph of a dense human audience on the left, and a row of hand-drawn robot-like characters on the right, visually staging the core metaphor of human audiences paired with machine agents. The floating panel on the right shows what looks like the actual interface of the system being pitched — character cards labeled "Remi / RECOMBINABILITY" and "Dex / D

Environment: A code editor (VS Code) with a running Electron application, showing an active development session for the same "storysymbiosis" project whose pitch slide was visible previously. The scene has shifted decisively from presentation-crafting to engineering.

A `.env` file is open in the top editor pane, listing placeholder slots for GEMINI_API_KEY, OPENAI_API_KEY, and ANTHROPIC_API_KEY — revealing that the system is designed to orchestrate multiple frontier LLMs simultaneously, likely routing different character agents to different models. A modal error dialog dominates the center: "A JavaScript error occurred in the main process — Uncaught Exception: Error: write EIO," traced into `main.js:80:13`, suggesting a stream/pipe failure in the Electron main process. Two terminal panes below show parallel processes: `npm start` launching the Electron frontend (which initially fail
